title: Calls to async methods should not be blocking in Azure Functions
description: Making blocking calls to async methods transforms the code into a synchronous operation. Doing so inside an Azure Function can lead to thread pool exhaustion. Thread pool exhaustion refers to a situation where all available threads in a thread pool are occupied, and new tasks or work items cannot be scheduled for execution due to the lack of available threads.
recommendation: Replace with the recommended alternative approach.

checks.message: Do not block on async methods in Azure Functions - use await to prevent thread pool exhaustion
